technology.title: Hunan Normal University Team Models Giant Atom Dynamics For Waveguide Quantum Emission
technology.desc: Until now, a giant atom’s radiative behaviour has differed from that of its smaller counterparts, exhibiting population trapping when coupling points were closely spaced. However, calculations demonstrate that when the distance, *d*, between these coupling points exceeds the coherent length, *L*, the giant atom’s dynamics become Markovian, mirroring a small atom’s response. This control over spontaneous emission, achieved by adjusting energy spacing and time delay, expands possibilities for manipulating quantum emitters within waveguide systems.

technology.title: Fuchs-Sondheimer Theory Fails As Films Drop Below Few Nanometers
technology.desc: Ultra-thin films, essential for nanoelectronics, experience increased electrical resistivity as thickness decreases to the few-nanometer scale. Current interpretations based on the Fuchs-Sondheimer theory are proving inadequate as films reach this extreme confinement, necessitating a shift toward understanding quantum-confinement effects.

technology.title: Quasi-Momentum Distribution Function Computed For First Time
technology.desc: Researchers at the University of Bologna have, for the first time, analytically computed the quasi-momentum distribution function for systems mapping to free fermions. This work addresses the behavior of integrable systems following a quantum quench, particularly those with an exponential degeneracy of energy eigenvalues.

technology.title: Scientists discover a protein switch that burns fat and blocks new fat cells
technology.desc: A protein called “Mitch” may hold the key to a new generation of obesity treatments. The findings help explain why mice lacking Mitch were leaner, more athletic, and resistant to obesity.

technology.title: Leaked video shows Microsoft’s work on a lightweight ‘Copilot OS’ built for AI.
technology.desc:
Zac Bowden at Windows Central posted this video of Aion, a stripped-down Windows concept made for agentic AI that popped up on the BetaWiki Discord, and says sources have confirmed to him that it is a real video made in 2024.
There’s no indication whether an OS like this would actually ship or if it’s part of something like Project Solara, but it’s very Chrome OS-like, built around the Edge browser and web apps.
[Media: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rj6wm0fl3PU]

technology.title: Quaid-I-Azam University Team Models Positronium Decay For Weak Process Investigation
technology.desc: Until now, the weak decay of positronium has remained a shadowy counterpart to its better-understood radiative cousin. Calculations reveal the branching ratio for positronium’s decay into an electron and muon neutrinos is comparable to that of ortho-positronium decaying into photons, opening a new avenue for probing three-body weak interactions within a bound state. This work establishes a theoretical foundation for understanding this previously less-studied process.
